questions.q: In the episode of the lifting of Govardhan, which deity raises the hill to shelter the cowherds?
questions.explanation: According to the Bhagavata Purana, young Krishna lifted Govardhan hill to protect the people and cattle of Vrindavan.

questions.q: What does Krishna lift in the famous episode set in Vrindavan?
questions.explanation: Krishna lifted the hill called Govardhan to shield the cowherds from a fierce storm.

questions.q: On which finger does Krishna hold up Govardhan hill, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Krishna balanced the entire Govardhan hill upon his little finger.

questions.q: Whose worship did Krishna persuade the cowherds to replace with the honoring of Govardhan hill?
questions.explanation: Krishna urged the cowherds to worship Govardhan hill instead of the rain-god Indra.

questions.q: In which region does the lifting of Govardhan hill by Krishna take place?
questions.explanation: The episode unfolds in Vrindavan, the pastoral land of the cowherds near Mathura.

questions.q: Whom did Krishna shelter beneath the lifted Govardhan hill?
questions.explanation: Krishna held up Govardhan to protect the cowherds of Vrindavan and their cattle from the storm.

questions.q: What did the angry Indra unleash upon Vrindavan when its people honored Govardhan hill instead of him?
questions.explanation: Indra sent a torrential, devastating rainstorm against Vrindavan, prompting Krishna to lift the hill.

questions.q: Krishna, who lifted Govardhan hill, is regarded in the tradition as an avatar of which deity?
questions.explanation: Krishna is venerated as an avatar of Vishnu, and the Govardhan episode reveals his divinity.

questions.q: What was the occupation of the people Krishna protected by lifting Govardhan hill?
questions.explanation: The folk of Vrindavan sheltered by Krishna were cowherds (gopas) who tended cattle.

questions.q: Which sacred text narrates the story of Krishna lifting Govardhan hill?
questions.explanation: The lifting of Govardhan is recounted in the Bhagavata Purana's account of Krishna's youth.

questions.q: For how many days does Krishna hold up Govardhan hill in the Bhagavata Purana account?
questions.explanation: Krishna held the hill aloft for seven days and nights until Indra finally relented.

questions.q: Which title, meaning 'lifter of Govardhan,' is given to Krishna for this deed?
questions.explanation: Krishna is honored as Govardhanadhari (also Giridhari), 'the one who lifted the mountain.'

questions.q: What domain or power is the god Indra, who sent the storm against Vrindavan, chiefly associated with in the Vedas?
options: The ocean and waters of the deep
questions.explanation: Indra is the Vedic king of the gods, wielder of the thunderbolt and lord of rain and storms.

questions.q: What was the elder brother of Krishna, present during his Vrindavan days, named?
questions.explanation: Balarama was Krishna's elder brother who shared his pastoral childhood in Vrindavan.

questions.q: What is the name of the foster-mother who raised Krishna in Vrindavan, where he lifted Govardhan?
questions.explanation: Yashoda, wife of the cowherd chief Nanda, lovingly raised Krishna in Vrindavan.

questions.q: What is the name of the cowherd chief and foster-father of Krishna in the Govardhan story?
questions.explanation: Nanda, the chief of the cowherds, was Krishna's foster-father in Vrindavan.

questions.q: How did the episode of lifting Govardhan affect Indra's view of Krishna in the Bhagavata Purana?
options: Indra waged endless war against Krishna
Indra relented and acknowledged Krishna's divinity
Indra cursed the people of Vrindavan
questions.explanation: After seven days Indra relented, his pride humbled, and acknowledged Krishna's divinity.

questions.q: The Govardhan episode is part of which broader phase of Krishna's life?
options: His role as charioteer at Kurukshetra
His reign as king of Dwarka
His final ascent from the world
questions.explanation: The lifting of Govardhan belongs to Krishna's pastoral childhood among the cowherds of Vrindavan.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, what reason did Krishna give for honoring Govardhan hill rather than Indra?
options: The hill was hollow and hid a treasure
The hill was the abode of Yama
The hill provided pasture, water and sustenance to the cattle
questions.explanation: Krishna taught that Govardhan deserved honor because it nourished the cattle with grass, water and shelter.

questions.q: Which weapon is most famously associated with Indra, the god who sent the storm upon Vrindavan?
questions.explanation: Indra wields the Vajra, the mighty thunderbolt, as told throughout the Vedas and Puranas.

questions.q: What festival celebrating Krishna's lifting of Govardhan is observed, especially in the Braj region?
questions.explanation: Govardhan Puja, also called Annakut, commemorates Krishna's lifting of the hill and is celebrated in Braj.

questions.q: Which discus, the emblem of Vishnu, is wielded by Krishna who is venerated as Vishnu's avatar?
questions.explanation: Krishna, as an avatar of Vishnu, bears the Sudarshana Chakra, Vishnu's spinning discus.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, near which sacred river does Vrindavan, the setting of the Govardhan episode, lie?
questions.explanation: Vrindavan lies along the banks of the Yamuna, where Krishna spent his pastoral childhood.

questions.q: After Indra relented, which celestial cow is said in the Bhagavata Purana to have joined in consecrating Krishna, giving rise to his name Govinda?
options: Surabhi, the celestial mother of cows
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that Surabhi, the celestial mother of cows, together with Indra consecrated Krishna as Govinda after Indra's submission.

questions.q: By which other name, meaning 'holder of the hill,' is Krishna known for lifting Govardhan?
questions.explanation: Krishna is called Giridhari, 'the bearer of the mountain,' for holding up Govardhan hill.

questions.q: Which serpent demon did Krishna subdue in the Yamuna during his Vrindavan childhood, in another episode from this period?
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavata Purana, Krishna danced upon and subdued the venomous serpent Kaliya in the Yamuna.

questions.q: What is the name of Indra's celestial elephant mount, ridden by the god who challenged Krishna at Govardhan?
questions.explanation: Indra rides the white elephant Airavata, which rose from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: The lifting of Govardhan is often read as Krishna humbling the pride of Indra and turning devotion toward what?
options: Direct devotion to Krishna and the nurturing land
questions.explanation: The episode redirects devotion from ritual appeasement of Indra toward Krishna and the nurturing land of Govardhan.

questions.q: In the cosmic scheme of the Puranas, Krishna's avatar belongs to which of the traditional dashavatara (ten avatars) of Vishnu?
options: He is an avatar of Brahma
He is counted among the ten principal avatars of Vishnu
He is an avatar of Shiva
He is one of the Ashvini twins
questions.explanation: Krishna is reckoned among Vishnu's principal avatars in the dashavatara tradition of the Puranas.

questions.q: Which uncle and tyrant-king of Mathura, who sought Krishna's life, is a central adversary of Krishna's youth around the time of the Vrindavan episodes?
questions.explanation: Kamsa, the tyrant-king of Mathura and Krishna's maternal uncle, sought to kill him throughout his youth.
